narrative:
DRIVER
USD ranks first on D1 CSM at 100 with CHF at 92 in a Risk-Off High regime, driven by the US 10Y surging 3% on inflation fears and the S&P 500 dropping 1.24%, forcing capital into safe-haven flows. USDCAD is the cleanest expression with D1 Strong Buy, 1212 CMP at 64, and D1 momentum accelerating at 72, while GBPUSD offers the sharpest short given GBP sits dead last on D1 CSM at 0 and GBPJPY correlation at 0.93 amplifies any GBP deterioration. CAD divergence is the critical flag: H4 CSM 100 versus D1 CSM 41 is a 59-point gap, meaning short-term CAD strength in USDCAD faces a near-term pullback risk before the D1 trend reasserts.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: USDCAD CMP 64, D1 72 accelerating, H4 66 confirmed uptrend on both timeframes
Fading: GBPUSD CMP 31 D1 and H4 both collapsing, AUDUSD CMP 41 H4 at 14 accelerating lower, NZDUSD CMP 43 H4 at 21 fading hard

WATCH
Iran-Oman mediation progress removes geopolitical premium and hits WTI, dragging CADJPY lower as oil correlation unwinds CAD's H4 strength advantage
Fed commentary reinforcing rate hike expectations pushes USDJPY through the H4 Buy signal toward D1 Strong Buy continuation above current range
ECB uncertainty event triggers EURUSD breakdown below D1 support, confirmed by USDCHF inverse move given the 0.79 negative correlation locking the pairs together

themes.theme: Oil supply concerns and Middle East tensions supporting risk-off sentiment
Iran-Oman mediation talks reducing immediate geopolitical premium
Bond yield surge on inflation fears pressuring equities and supporting USD
Fed rate hike expectations supporting USD despite near-term volatility
Emerging market currencies weakening on oil price shock and risk-off flows
EUR weakness amid ECB uncertainty and broader bond sell-off
GBP technical pressure below 200-DMA despite near-term bounces
Safe-haven flows supporting JPY and CHF despite oil-driven pressures

key_observation: USD Index trading near 99.15 supported by Fed rate hike expectations and bond yield strength despite temporary relief from Iran-Oman mediation talks. Oil prices remain elevated around 101.50 USD/bbl with supply concerns from potential Hormuz disruption. Risk-off sentiment evident in EM currency weakness particularly INR and IDR hitting record lows. Equity markets under pressure from surging bond yields creating cross-asset volatility.
watch: Iran-Pakistan mediation progress on Hormuz stability, oil inventory drawdown trajectory, US Treasury yield direction and Fed communications, emerging market currency support levels, equities stabilization attempts
